untranslatable_reason: Broadening Formation pattern fundamentally relies on visual identification of expanding price envelope (wider highs AND wider lows over time) and measure rule projections. The pattern's defining characteristic—expanding volatility bands with increasing failure rates at minor target extensions—cannot be reliably encoded into static indicator thresholds. The 'failure rate increases substantially with minor increases in targets' metric requires complex statistical validation of projected levels vs. actual price action, which exceeds simple indicator conditions. Recommend manual chart review or proprietary broadening detection algorithm.
